structured.description: A class called "Calibration" that performs calibration for a robotic arm using a camera attached to it. The code first imports necessary packages, including numpy, scipy, and pybullet. It then defines functions for detecting corners in an image, calculating the error between real and virtual corners, and minimizing the error function using the Nelder-Mead method to find the optimal parameters for the camera intrinsic matrix. The code also includes a summary of the calibration process and writes the results to a file.

items.description: Performs calibration of a kinova cube robot using pybullet library and OpenCV for image processing. It detects green corners, compares them with real ones, calculates errors and minimizes them through a non-linear optimization algorithm.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description: 1280 pixels, which represents the width of the camera image used for calibration.

attributes.name: error
attributes.description: 0 at first, then it gets updated with the error calculation during the calibration process. It represents the total error between the estimated and real positions of the corners.

attributes.name: real_corners
attributes.description: 4D, representing the real corners of the object in the image. It contains the coordinates of the real corners detected using the green rectangle detection algorithm.

attributes.name: robot_id
attributes.description: Used to identify the specific robot being calibrated during the process.

attributes.name: observation_angles
attributes.description: 7-dimensional, containing the angles for each joint in a robot in radians.

items.name: Calibrator

items.description: Sets the parameters for a calibration process, such as camera intrinsic and extrinsic parameters, and the angles of observation.
items.name: __init__

items.description: Determines whether a set of 4 points can be formed into a rectangle based on the cosine of the angles between adjacent pairs of points. If all cosine values are below a threshold (default 0.1), it returns True, otherwise it returns False.

params.name: approx
params.description: Used to represent an approximate rectangle.

returns.description: True if the given approximate rectangle is a rectangle and False otherwise.

items.name: is_rectangle

items.description: Calculates the angle between two points in a 2D space based on their distances and angles relative to a reference point.

params.name: pt1
params.description: Representing a point on a two-dimensional plane.

params.name: pt2
params.description: Represented by a tuple containing the x and y coordinates, respectively.

params.name: pt0
params.description: Represented as a point (x,y) value.

returns.description: The angle between two points based on their distances and directions from a reference point.

items.name: angle

items.description: Detects green regions in an image using HSV color space, then finds contours within those regions and extracts corner points.

params.name: image
params.description: Used to represent the input image for corner detection.

returns.description: A collection of corner points detected from an image using Hough transform.

items.name: detect_green_corners

items.description: Computes the distance between real and virtual corners, calculates the error, and returns the sum of errors.

params.name: params
params.description: 7-dimensional, containing the 3 real corners and 4 virtual corners of an object.

returns.description: 1000 times the distance between the real and virtual corners of a rectangle.

items.name: error_function

items.description: In the Calibrator class calculates the distance between real and virtual corners, checks for errors, and updates the errors array.

params.name: params
params.description: 7-dimensional, containing 3 real values, followed by 4 integer indices.

returns.description: The sum of the norms of the differences between the detected virtual corners and the real corners.

items.name: error_function2

items.description: Performs corner detection and matching between a set of real corners detected by a previous step, and a set of virtual corners obtained from a green-texture image processed by the `cvPose2BulletView` method. The function calculates the errors between the real and virtual corners, and returns the sum of these errors as an integer value.

params.name: params
params.description: Used to store the camera translation value.

returns.description: Not a valid error value, indicating an error in the code.

items.name: error_function3

items.description: Performs camera calibration for both Kinova and Pybullet cameras. It detects rectangles in the images, computes offsets for the real corners, and updates the position of the camera parameters.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: Used to represent the left-hand side (LHS) of the calibration equation, which is the image of the object in the robot's camera.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot for which the calibration is performed.

items.name: calibrate

items.description:
Computes the optimal position and orientation of a robot based on image data.
It uses a minimization algorithm to find the solution that reduces an error function.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: 2D array that stores the raw image captured by the Kinova camera.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot being calibrated.

items.name: calibrate2

items.description: Within the Calibrator class takes robot ID, an image from Kinova, and initial camera parameters as inputs. It then computes the fixed projection matrix using the green corners detection method and minimizes the error function to find the optimal translation vector for the camera. Finally, it displays the detected corners in both Kinova and Pybullet images.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to retrieve link state data from the Kinova robot controller.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: A grayscale image representing the left side camera view.

items.name: calibrate3

items.description: Computes the view matrix and projection matrix for a camera using a FOV, aspect ratio, near plane, and far plane. It then rotates the image and converts it to BGR format before returning it.

returns.description: A RGB image represented as a numpy array.

items.name: read_camera

items.description: Computes and returns the fixed camera matrix and view matrix for a given camera, based on its intrinsic and extrinsic parameters.

returns.description: A grayscale image representing the scene captured by a camera.

items.name: read_camera_fixed

items.description: Within the `Calibrator` class detects and matches green corners between two images, calculating the distance between them using norms, and returns the matched corners and errors in a matrix format.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot to which the code is applied.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: An image captured by a camera linked to a robot.

items.name: cube_test

items.description: Within the Calibrator class performs rectangle detection on two images: one from a Pybullert camera and another from a Kinova camera. It matches virtual corners with real corners using a descriptor-based approach and calculates errors between the two sets of corners.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to specify the robot ID for which the rectangle detection should be performed.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: Passed as an argument to the function for testing purposes.

items.name: square_test

items.description: Within the Calibrator class retrieves images from a Kinova robot and a camera, moves the robot's joints to specific angles, and saves the obtained color images to a directory.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the specific robot being controlled.

params.name: kinova_proxy
params.description: Used to control the movement of a kinova arm.

params.name: camera_proxy
params.description: Used to get all images from the camera.

items.name: get_kinova_images

items.description: Calculates the error between real and virtual corners in a 2D image, given by a set of parameters. It returns the total error as a scalar value.

params.name: params
params.description: 3-dimensional, containing the following values: optical center x pixels, optical center y pixels, and focal length in pixels.

returns.description: The sum of the absolute differences between the virtual corners and real corners found using SIFT feature detection and matching.

items.name: error_function4_square

items.description: Calculates the distance between real and virtual corners and returns the error. It also detects green corners from an image and compares them with the expected ones.

params.name: params
params.description: Passed as a single argument to the function. It contains two elements: `optical_center_x_pixels` and `optical_center_y_pixels`, which are the coordinates of the optical center in pixels.

returns.description: 2000 if there are at least 1000 differences between the real and virtual corners, otherwise it returns the number of differences.

items.name: error_function4_cube

items.description: Within the `Calibrator` class minimizes an error function to find the optimal parameters for a calibration process using the Nelder-Mead method.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Assigned the value passed to the function as an argument, serving as an identifier for the robot being calibrated.

items.name: calibrate4

structured.description: A robot arm with 7 joints and moves it based on user input from a joystick connected to a computer. The code uses the PyRobot library for Robot Operating System (ROS) to interface with the robot arm and move its end effector to specific positions. It also calculates the inverse kinematics solution (joint positions) to reach the desired position of the end effector based on the user input. Finally, it sets the joint motor control using ROS to move the robot arm to the desired position.

items.description: Controls a robot's joint movements based on user input from a joystick, with various move modes and button presses to change the robot's behavior.

attributes.name: Period
attributes.description: 1000 by default, representing the sampling period of the worker's data in milliseconds.

attributes.name: rgb
attributes.description: 3D, representing the red, green, and blue colors of the worker's body, respectively.

attributes.name: startup_check
attributes.description: 0 by default, indicating that the worker has not been started yet. It is used to check if the worker has been started or not during the execution of the program.

attributes.name: physicsClient
attributes.description: Used to interact with the Bullet physics engine to handle the robot's movements, collisions, and other physical interactions.

attributes.name: plane
attributes.description: Not documented.

attributes.name: table_id
attributes.description: Used to store a unique identifier for each worker in the table. It is used internally by the `worker` class to keep track of the workers and their movements.

attributes.name: robot_urdf
attributes.description:
Used to store the robot's URDF (Universal Robot Description Format) file, which defines its 
kinematic structure and can be used for motion planning and control.

attributes.name: robot_launch_pos
attributes.description: 4D, representing the position of the robot's end effector (gripper) in the world coordinate system.

attributes.name: robot_launch_orien
attributes.description: 3x3 rotation matrix representing the orientation of the robot's end effector after launching the payload.

attributes.name: end_effector_link_index
attributes.description: 7 by default, which corresponds to the index of the end effector link in the robot's kinematic tree. It determines which joint or link of the robot the end effector is attached to.

attributes.name: home_angles
attributes.description: 5x1 numpy array containing the angles of the robot's joints to return to their home position when the "home" button is pressed.

attributes.name: observation_angles
attributes.description: 7-length vector of angles representing the joints' angles that are observed by the robot.

attributes.name: observation_angles_2
attributes.description: 2D, containing the joint angles and their corresponding observations for the second robot.

attributes.name: observation_angles_3
attributes.description: 3-dimensional, representing the angles of the robot's end effector (joints) observed through a kinematic model. It is used to calculate the robot's position based on the joint angles.

attributes.name: observation_angles_cube
attributes.description: 3D array containing the angles for each joint of the robot, in radians, observed by the worker through its sensors.

attributes.name: robot_id
attributes.description: 4 by default, indicating that the worker is a Kinova robot with 4 joints. It serves as an identifier for the specific robot model being controlled by the worker.

attributes.name: pybullet_cup
attributes.description: Used to represent a specific cup shape for a robot arm in pybullet simulations.

attributes.name: square
attributes.description: 4 by default, which indicates that the worker's gripper has a square-shaped jaw.

attributes.name: hilo_lectura
attributes.description: 0 by default, which indicates that the worker is reading data from the HIL (Hardware-in-the-Loop) system.

attributes.name: readDataFromProxy
attributes.description: Used to read data from a PyRobot2 proxy server through a socket connection. It retrieves joint angles, button states, and other robot's internal state data from the proxy server.

attributes.name: hilo_camara
attributes.description: 0-based indexing for the joints of a specific robot, indicating the position of the camera in relation to the robot's joints.

attributes.name: readCamera
attributes.description: Defined as a function that reads camera data from a PyRobot library file.

attributes.name: target_angles
attributes.description: 1-D, representing the target angles (in radians) for each joint of a kinematic robotic manipulator in the move mode specified by `move_mode`.

attributes.name: target_position
attributes.description:
Stored as a list of three elements representing the desired position of the end effector 
of the robot in joint space. It is updated based on the user input provided by the 
`JoystickAdapter` class.

attributes.name: target_orientation
attributes.description: Used to store the desired orientation of the end effector (gripper) of a robot in the simulation environment, which can be set using the `setTargetOrientation()` method.

attributes.name: target_velocities
attributes.description: 1x3 array representing the target velocities for each joint of the robot in radians per second.

attributes.name: joy_selected_joint
attributes.description: 0-based, it keeps track of the selected joint by the user input from the joystick, where 0 represents the first joint, 1 represents the second joint and so on.

attributes.name: move_mode
attributes.description: 0 by default, it represents the current mode of movement of the robot (0: move forward, 1: move backward, 2: move left, 3: move right, 4: home).

attributes.name: n_rotations
attributes.description: 5 by default, which represents the number of rotational degrees that the robot's end effector can move per one full rotation of its joints.

attributes.name: ext_joints
attributes.description: Used to store the joint angles of the robot's end effector in radians, which are calculated based on the user input data from the joystick.

attributes.name: kinovaarm_proxy
attributes.description: A proxy for the KINOVA arm, which allows it to interact with the PyBullet simulation environment.

attributes.name: ext_gripper
attributes.description: 4-dimensional, representing the target position and orientation of a gripper attached to the robot's end effector. It is used to store the desired pose of the gripper in the robot's world frame of reference.

attributes.name: posesTimes
attributes.description: 1D array containing time stamps (in milliseconds) for each joint's position.

attributes.name: poses
attributes.description: 1D array containing the current joint angles of the robot in radians, represented as a list of 7 values representing the angles of each joint.

attributes.name: timestamp
attributes.description: 0 by default, representing the current timestamp (in milliseconds) since the start of the simulation. It is used to store the time at which each command is sent to the robot.

attributes.name: timer
attributes.description: Used to store the time taken by the worker to execute its task.

attributes.name: compute
attributes.description:
Responsible for computing the joint angles that will move the end effector to a specific position 
and orientation. It takes in data from the joystick adapter and computes the angles based on the 
current move mode, home position, target position, and orientation.

attributes.name: timer2
attributes.description: 0 by default, which indicates that the worker has been running for 2 seconds. It is used to manage the worker's execution time.

attributes.name: movePybulletWithExternalVel
attributes.description: Used to move a robot with external velocity input from a joystick adapter. It takes in the joystick data as input and updates the robot's position, orientation, and gripper position based on the input values.

attributes.name: joint_speeds
attributes.description: 7-element vector representing the target joint angles in radians for each of the 7 joints of the robot, which are used to compute the end-effector pose and move the robot accordingly.

attributes.name: gains
attributes.description: 7-element array storing the gains for each joint of the robot, representing the control gains for the robot's joints.

attributes.name: speeds
attributes.description: Used to store the speed values for each joint in the robot, which are used to control the robot's movement.

attributes.name: angles
attributes.description: 1-D array containing the joint angles of the robot in radians, representing the current state of the robot.

attributes.name: timer3
attributes.description: 0 by default, it is used to keep track of the time spent executing the code inside the `if __name__ == "__main__"` block.

attributes.name: moveKinovaWithSpeeds
attributes.description: Responsible for moving the robotic arm with specified speeds based on the received joystick data. It updates the joint angles, gripper position, and orientation based on the move mode, axis values, and buttons pressed in the joystick.

attributes.name: timer4
attributes.description: 4, indicating that the worker is a SpecificWorker with move mode 4, which controls the movement of the robot's end effector.

attributes.name: movePybulletWithToolbox
attributes.description: Used to send move commands to a pybullet environment. It takes in a `data` parameter which contains the joystick data, and then sends the appropriate commands based on the mode, axes, and buttons of the joystick.

attributes.name: colorKinova
attributes.description: Used to represent the color of the robot's kinova joints.

attributes.name: depthKinova
attributes.description: 3D depth map of the Kinova robot in meters, obtained through the PyBullet module.

attributes.name: calibrator
attributes.description: Used to store the calibration data for the specific worker, which is used to adjust the robot's end effector positions based on user inputs.

attributes.name: timer5
attributes.description: 5 by default, which represents the time interval between consecutive calls to the `sendData()` method of the joystick adapter.

attributes.name: readKinovaCamera
attributes.description: Used to read the camera images from the Kinova robot. It returns a tuple containing the (x, y) coordinates of the detected objects in the image.

items.name: SpecificWorker

items.description: Initializes variables and connections for moving the Kinova arm through PyBullet, reading camera images, and calibrating the robot's end effector positions and orientations based on the target angles provided.

params.name: proxy_map
params.description: Used to map Python-side variables to C++-side objects, allowing for interaction between the two sides.

params.name: startup_check
params.description: Used to check if the robot is in startup mode or not. If it's in startup mode, it will run the `startup_check` function.

items.name: __init__

items.description: Sets parameters for an object of its class, returning `True`.

params.name: params
params.description: Used to set parameters for an instance of the `JavaClass` class.

returns.description: The outcome of applying the operation to the input given.

items.name: setParams

items.description: Performs calibration for a specific robot arm (defined by `robot_id`) using a kinematic model, and then moves the arm to a fixed position using the angles stored in `home_angles`. It also initializes toolbox variables and starts a timer for the movement.
items.name: compute

items.description: Tests various components and methods of the `ifaces` module, including `RoboCompKinovaArm`, `TGripper`, `TJoint`, `TJoints`, `RoboCompJoystickAdapter`, and others. It uses print statements to output the results of each test.
items.name: startup_check

items.description: Initializes various components of the worker's environment, including grippers, axes, and a cup object, and sets the desired end-effector pose for the worker.
items.name: inicializar_toolbox

items.description: Computes joint velocities for a robot based on torque and velocity limits, and updates the joint angles to approach the goal position while respecting constraints.
items.name: toolbox_compute

items.description: Computes and applies a view matrix to obtain an image from a camera, and then displays the resulting RGB image using OpenCV's `imshow` function.
items.name: readCamera

items.description: Retrieves camera extrinsic and intrinsic parameters using a Kalman filter, then combines them to form a view matrix for rendering an image.

returns.description: 4D image with RGB channels and 3D transformation matrix for the camera.

items.name: read_camera_fixed

items.description: Retrieves image data from Kinova cameras and normalizes it for further processing. It returns `True` upon successful execution.

returns.description: 1 or more objects depending on the context.

items.name: readKinovaCamera

items.description: Computes and prints the radians of the joints of an object in Kinova format, using the angles provided by the `ext_joints` attribute of the class instance.
items.name: showKinovaAngles

items.description: Computes camera calibration for a Kinova arm using a set of images captured by the camera attached to the arm. It uses the Chessboard pattern to detect the corners of the images and computes the intrinsic parameters of the camera matrix.

returns.description: 1 if the calibration was successful, and 0 otherwise.

items.name: get_kinova_instrinsic

items.description: Prints four options for interacting with a displayed window: taking a picture, computing calibration, resetting the program, and quitting.
items.name: usage

items.description: Updates the velocity of a robot's joints based on external velocities provided, and sets motor control targets for the joints using PyBullet library.
items.name: movePybulletWithExternalVel

items.description: Controls the velocity of a robot using PyBullet, setting joint motor control values based on target velocities provided in a list.
items.name: movePybulletWithToolbox

items.description: Reads data from a Kinova arm proxy, obtaining joints and gripper states, and reduces the gripper distance by 80% before sleeping for 0.05 seconds.
items.name: readDataFromProxy

items.description: Moves the joints of a kinova arm based on angles provided as input. The angles are first converted to degrees and then passed to the `moveJointsWithAngle` method of the `kinovaarm_proxy` object, which controls the arm's movement.

params.name: angles
params.description: 360-degree angle values representing the joint angles of the Kinova arm to be moved.

items.name: moveKinovaWithAngles

items.description: Calculates and sets joint speeds for a Kinova arm based on joint angles and gain values, then sends the speeds to the Kinova arm proxy for movement.
items.name: moveKinovaWithSpeeds

items.description: Updates the gains of a robot's joints based on the difference between the actual angle and the target angle, and prints the current gains and error values to the console.
items.name: updateGains

items.description: Processes data from a joystick and updates the position and orientation of a robot based on the received commands.

params.name: data
params.description: Passed as an argument to the function. It contains data from a joystick, including axis values and button states.

items.name: JoystickAdapter_sendData
